tools.name: MagicSchool AI
tools.description: An AI suite built for teachers, with 60+ tools covering lesson planning, differentiation, report writing, and student feedback. Widely used in NZ schools.

notes.cost: Free for teachers. Paid plans add extra features but the free tier is substantial.
notes.privacy: US-based. Claims FERPA and COPPA compliance. Review their student data policy before use with under-18s.
notes.guardrails: Built specifically for education. Content is curriculum-appropriate throughout.
notes.accessibility: Functional but not fully WCAG 2.1 AA certified.

tools.name: Diffit
tools.description: Generates differentiated reading materials at any level from a URL, topic, or your own text. Particularly useful for adapting complex texts for diverse learners.

notes.cost: Free tier available with usage limits. Paid plans from around $10/month.
notes.privacy: US-based. Limited transparency in privacy policy. Avoid entering personally identifiable student information.
notes.guardrails: Content is focused on curriculum adaptation. Output is appropriate for classroom use.
notes.accessibility: Basic interface. Screen reader support is limited.

tools.name: ChatGPT
tools.description: OpenAI's general-purpose AI assistant. Highly capable for brainstorming, drafting, and explanation. Not designed specifically for education, so requires teacher guidance on appropriate use.

notes.cost: Free plan available. ChatGPT Plus is $20/month. ChatGPT Edu plans exist for institutions.
notes.privacy: US-based. Conversations may be used for model training unless opted out. Not FERPA/COPPA compliant without a specific education agreement.
notes.guardrails: Content filters exist but this is a general-purpose tool. Needs explicit classroom norms to support responsible use.
notes.accessibility: Good WCAG 2.1 AA compliance. Works well with screen readers.

tools.name: Claude.ai
tools.description: Anthropic's AI assistant. Strong at nuanced writing, analysis, and reasoning. Notably strong privacy defaults compared to other general AI tools.

notes.cost: Free plan available. Claude Pro is $20/month. Free tier has usage limits.
notes.privacy: Conversations are not used for model training by default. Strong privacy stance from Anthropic.
notes.guardrails: Strong safety training but a general-purpose tool. Establish clear classroom norms for student use.
notes.accessibility: Good accessibility. Clean interface works well with assistive technology.

tools.name: Khanmigo
tools.description: Khan Academy's AI tutor. Uses Socratic questioning rather than giving direct answers, making it a strong model for learning. Education guardrails are the strongest of any tool here.

notes.cost: Free for teachers via Khan Academy. Student access requires a subscription ($9/month) unless income-qualified.
notes.privacy: COPPA and FERPA compliant. Strong data protection policies. One of the safest options for student data.
notes.guardrails: Purpose-built for education. Refuses to do homework for students, uses guided questioning instead.
notes.accessibility: Khan Academy has strong accessibility standards. Works well with screen readers and keyboard navigation.

tools.name: Curipod
tools.description: Generates interactive lessons with polls, word clouds, and drawing activities. Good for discussion and formative assessment hooks.

notes.cost: Free for educators. Generous free tier.
notes.privacy: European company, GDPR compliant. Student responses are collected and stored. Review data retention settings.
notes.guardrails: Education platform with moderated content. Appropriate for classroom use.
notes.accessibility: Interactive elements may present challenges for some assistive technologies.

tools.name: Canva
tools.description: Design platform with strong AI features including image generation, text-to-presentation, and Magic Write. Free Education plan available for verified teachers and students.

notes.cost: Education plan is free for verified teachers and students (Canva for Education).
notes.privacy: Australian company (listed in US). Data used for product improvement. Opt-out options are limited.
notes.guardrails: Content filters on AI image generation but it is a general-use platform. Image generation can produce unexpected results.
notes.accessibility: Strong accessibility features. Good screen reader support and keyboard navigation.

tools.name: Grammarly
tools.description: AI writing assistant for grammar, clarity, and style suggestions. The free tier is useful for polishing student writing. Honest limitation: it focuses on surface-level language, not ideas.

notes.cost: Free tier covers grammar and basic suggestions. Pro is $30/month. Education plans available.
notes.privacy: US-based. Text is sent to Grammarly servers for processing. Review privacy settings before using with student writing.
notes.guardrails: Writing assistance only. Does not generate harmful content. Safe scope of use.
notes.accessibility: Browser extension works well but the standalone app has some accessibility gaps.

tools.name: NotebookLM
tools.description: Google's AI research tool that lets you upload documents and ask questions of them. Useful for helping students analyse texts or for teachers synthesising research. Audio overview feature generates podcast-style summaries.

notes.cost: Free to use with a Google account.
notes.privacy: Subject to Google's standard data policies. Use Google Workspace for Education accounts for stronger student data protections.
notes.guardrails: Grounded in the documents you upload, which reduces hallucination risk. Still a general-purpose tool.
notes.accessibility: Google's standard accessibility, which is generally strong.
